title: Dashboard report
description: Data contract for the dashboard format: a monitoring / operations console. The page is three structural zones — a fixed slim left icon nav-rail, a top KPI band of 4-6 stat tiles (big tnum numbers, deltas, sparks), and a 12-column CSS grid of rounded widget panels (gauges, mini bar charts, spark lines, status lists, compact tables). No prose, no TOC; designed to be scanned in three seconds. Renders to a full-viewport, self-contained HTML console. The sample's hardcoded content is a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome (org / project / title / theme). Brand-neutral: use real or brand-neutral values (e.g. Independent Research / Open Research / Research Desk); never leave a {{...}} placeholder.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label shown in the topbar, e.g. 'Platform reliability'.

title.description: Console title shown in the topbar, e.g. 'Open Research · Operations Console'.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line sub-title or team context.

date.description: ISO date / as-of timestamp the snapshot was generated (YYYY-MM-DD).

environment.description: Optional environment chip shown in the topbar, e.g. 'PROD · ap-northeast-1'.

theme.description: Initial brand theme; applied as <html data-theme>. Dashboard default is 'blue'.

navItems.description: Icon-only navigation buttons stacked in the left rail. Each item is a nav destination or view toggle; render order is top-to-bottom. One item should be marked active on load.

id.description: Stable unique key for the nav item, used to wire the active state.

label.description: Tooltip / accessible label for the icon button, e.g. 'Overview'.

icon.description: Icon category key from the house icon set. Dashboard-relevant categories: 'data' (charts, metrics), 'status' (health, incidents), 'nav' (home, back, settings), 'infra' (server, cluster, deploy), 'time' (clock, schedule, history).

active.description: If true, this button receives the is-active highlight on load. At most one item should set this.

kpis.description: The KPI band: a single row of 4-6 stat tiles rendered directly above the widget grid. Each tile = a mono uppercase label + big tnum number + optional delta + optional inline spark. These are the at-a-glance headline numbers.

id.description: Optional stable key for the KPI tile.

label.description: Short mono uppercase label, e.g. 'Availability', 'Req / sec', 'Error rate'.

value.description: The displayed tnum value as a string (preserve formatting / units inline), e.g. '99.982%', '18.4k', '214ms'.

unit.description: Optional unit suffix appended in a smaller font after the value, e.g. '%', 'ms', 'k'. Alternatively include it in value.

delta.description: Optional delta label shown beneath the value, e.g. '+0.014', '+6.2%', '+11.3%'.

trend.description: Direction of the delta, used to colour the delta indicator. 'up' = ok colour (improving); 'down' = risk colour (worsening); 'flat' = neutral.

state.description: Semantic health state of this KPI tile; drives the tile's accent colour class (is-ok / is-hold / is-risk / none = neutral).

sloLabel.description: Optional secondary label shown alongside the delta, e.g. 'SLO 99.95', 'budget 200'.

spark.description: Optional inline spark-line data points (normalised 0-100 values, oldest first). Rendered as a tiny SVG polyline inside the KPI tile.

icon.description: Optional icon category key from the house set shown beside the label. Categories: 'data', 'status', 'infra', 'time'.

widgets.description: The widget grid: rounded panels tiled on a 12-column CSS grid. Each widget spans N columns via 'span' and renders one chart/list/table type. Order is spatial (not narrative); widgets can be read in any order.

type.description: Widget render type. 'gauge' = CSS/SVG ring or semicircle gauge (SLA / utilisation); 'barchart' = mini horizontal or vertical bar chart (latency, throughput by service); 'linechart' = spark/line trend chart (error rate, request volume); 'spark' = standalone spark trend (single metric history); 'statlist' = scannable service health list with st-badges; 'table' = compact incident / deploy data table.

title.description: Widget heading shown in the compact widget-head bar.

span.description: Number of 12-column grid columns this widget occupies (3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 12). Typical: gauge=3, spark=4, barchart/statlist=5, linechart=4, table=7.

meta.description: Optional tiny meta label shown right-aligned in the widget head, e.g. '30d', 'cluster', 'stream'.

data.description: Widget payload. Shape varies by type. gauge: {value:number, max:number, unit:string, state:ok|hold|risk, label:string}. barchart: {bars:[{label,value,max,state}]}. linechart/spark: {points:[0-100], labels:[]}. statlist: {rows:[{label,value,status:ok|hold|risk,meta}]}. table: {columns:[{key,label,numeric?}], rows:[{...cells, state:ok|hold|risk}]}.

statusList.description: Optional top-level service / system health list. If present, rendered as a widget or embedded in a statlist widget. Each entry maps to an st-badge row.

label.description: Service or target name shown in the row.

value.description: Optional primary value shown right-aligned, e.g. '99.9%', '42ms'.

meta.description: Optional micro trailing figure, e.g. a latency count or last-event time.

rows.summary: payments-api — p95 over budget · downstream slow
